prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a mostly plain, dark gray screen in black and white. The image has visible film grain and slight edge artifacts, but no discernible objects or people are visible.

Near the end, a graphic appears: a large white heart shape centered on a darker gray background. In the middle of the heart is a jar or container label reading “SKIPPY PEANUT BUTTER.” The product image is centered and presented as the main visual focus.

The final frame shows a closer view of the same product graphic. The Skippy peanut butter container or label fills much more of the frame, with the heart shape still visible behind it. The words “SKIPPY” and “PEANUT BUTTER” are clearly readable. The transition appears to be either a zoom-in or a cut to a closer shot; the sampled frames do not make the exact camera movement fully clear. No people or other settings are visible in the sampled frames.
